Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»BREAKING: Tatjana Smith posts fastest time in 200m breaststroke heats

Tatjana Smith wasted no time in living up to her billing as the favourite for the women’s 200m breaststroke gold medal at the Paris Olympics.

South Africa’sTatjana Smithwasted no time in living up to her billing as the favourite for the women’s200m breaststrokegold medal at theParis Olympics 2024on Wednesday morning.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Smith, swimming in Heat 1 of the event, posted thefastest overall timeof2:21:57.

Team South Africa’s second entrant in the event,Kaylene Corbett, finished second in the same heat as Smith in a time of2:23:85which wound up as thesixthfastest overall when all was said and done.

Smith is the defending Olympic champion in the 200m breaststroke having won in Tokyo in 2021.

Only23swimmers took to the starting blocks for the heats which saw the top 16 qualify for the semi-finals starting at21:51(SA time), also on Wednesday, 31 July.

The top 8 will race the final onThursday, 1 Augustat21:04(SA time).

Meanwhile, as reported byThe South African website, Tatjana Smith took victory in the women’s 100m breaststroke at the Paris Olympics 2024 on Monday evening, giving South Africa its first gold medal of the Games.

Smith touched the wall in a time of1:05:28, finishing ahead of China’sQianting Tang(1:05:54), with Ireland’sMona McSharryin third (1:05.59).

Tributes were quick to pour in, including fromchicken fast food chain Nando’s.

With her 100m breaststroke win, Smith becameSouth Africa’s greatest ever Olympianwith two gold medals and a silver (and counting …)

Should Smith win the 200m breaststroke gold medal,she would emulatecompatriotPenny Heynswho completed the 100m-200m breaststroke golden double at the 1996 Olympics in Atlanta.

Smith pocketedR400 000for her win in the women’s 100m breaststroke thanks to the financial incentives promised by theSouth African Sports Confederation and Olympic Committee (Sascoc).

Smith’s coach, Rocco Meiring, will earn R100 000 for his role’s in Smith’s victory.

IndividualsGold medallists R400 000 and the coach R100 000Silver medallists R200 000 and the coach R50 000Bronze medallists R75 000 and the coach R25 000

In addition, as reported byThe South African website, new Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture,Gayton McKenzie, promised that he has a ‘big surprise’ for all Team SA medal winners, suggesting there could be more prize money coming their way.

title: Breaking: Tatjana Smith posts fastest time 200m breaststroke heats
author: Garrin Lambley
description: Tatjana Smith wasted no time in living up to her billing as the favourite for the women's 200m breaststroke gold medal at the Paris Olympics.
